auth0-fastapi.title: Auth0FastAPI
auth0-fastapi.description:
The main class for protecting FastAPI routes with Auth0.

Create one instance per application and use `require_auth` to
guard individual routes. The instance exposes ``api_client`` for advanced
use cases such as token exchange.

**Single domain setup**

**Multiple custom domains setup**

constructor.signature: Auth0FastAPI(domain: Optional[str] = None, audience: str = '', domains: Union[list[str], Callable, NoneType] = None, client_id=None, client_secret=None, custom_fetch=None, dpop_enabled=True, dpop_required=False, dpop_iat_leeway=30, dpop_iat_offset=300, cache_adapter: Optional[auth0_api_python.cache.CacheAdapter] = None, cache_ttl_seconds: int = 600, cache_max_entries: int = 100)

parameters.name: domain
parameters.description: Your Auth0 tenant domain, for example ``"your-tenant.us.auth0.com"``. Use this for single-tenant setups. Omit when using ``domains``.

parameters.name: audience
parameters.description: The API identifier registered in the Auth0 Dashboard. This must match the ``aud`` claim in incoming tokens. Required.

parameters.name: domains
parameters.description: A list of allowed Auth0 domains, or a callable that resolves domains at request time. Use this for multi-tenant setups where tokens may come from different Auth0 tenants.

parameters.name: client_id
parameters.description: The client ID used for token exchange flows.

parameters.name: client_secret
parameters.description: The client secret used for token exchange flows.

parameters.name: custom_fetch
parameters.description: A custom async HTTP function to replace the default HTTP client used by the underlying SDK. Useful for testing or proxying.

parameters.name: dpop_enabled
parameters.description: Accept DPoP-bound tokens in addition to Bearer tokens. Defaults to ``True``.

parameters.name: dpop_required
parameters.description: When ``True``, only DPoP tokens are accepted. Requests using a plain Bearer token are rejected with a 401. Defaults to ``False``.

parameters.name: dpop_iat_leeway
parameters.description: Clock skew tolerance in seconds when validating the ``iat`` claim of a DPoP proof. Defaults to ``30``.

parameters.name: dpop_iat_offset
parameters.description: Maximum acceptable age of a DPoP proof in seconds. Proofs older than this value are rejected. Defaults to ``300``.

parameters.name: cache_adapter
parameters.description: A custom cache backend that implements `CacheAdapter`. When provided, ``cache_max_entries`` is ignored and you configure the cache directly on your adapter. Defaults to an in-memory LRU cache.

parameters.name: cache_ttl_seconds
parameters.description: How long JWKS results are cached in seconds. Defaults to ``600``.

parameters.name: cache_max_entries
parameters.description: Maximum number of entries in the default in-memory cache before the least-recently-used entry is evicted. Ignored when ``cache_adapter`` is provided. Defaults to ``100``.

members.title: require_auth
members.description:
Return a FastAPI dependency that validates the incoming request's access token.

Attach this to any route using FastAPI's ``Depends()``. The dependency
automatically detects whether the client is using a Bearer token or a
DPoP-bound token, validates it against Auth0, and returns the decoded
JWT claims on success.

If ``scopes`` are specified, the token's ``scope`` claim must contain
every scope in the list. A missing or insufficient scope returns a 403.

parameters.name: scopes
parameters.description: One or more OAuth2 scopes that the token must include. Pass a single string or a list of strings.

returns.description: An async dependency callable that resolves to a ``dict`` containing the decoded JWT claims (for example ``sub``, ``scope``, ``iss``, ``aud``).

throws.name: HTTPException
throws.description: When the token is missing, expired, or invalid.

throws.name: HTTPException
throws.description: When the token does not contain the required scopes.

throws.name: HTTPException
throws.description: When an unexpected error occurs during validation.

examples.code:
from auth0_fastapi_api import Auth0FastAPI

auth0 = Auth0FastAPI(
    domain="your-tenant.us.auth0.com",
    audience="https://api.example.com"
)

cache-adapter.title: CacheAdapter

members.title: delete
members.description:
Delete value from cache.

Args:
    key: Cache key to delete

members.title: get
members.description:
Get value from cache by key.

Args:
    key: Cache key to retrieve

Returns:
    Cached value if found and not expired, None otherwise

members.title: set
members.signature: set(key: str, value: Any, ttl_seconds: Optional[int] = None)
members.description:
Set value in cache with optional TTL.

Args:
    key: Cache key to store
    value: Value to cache
    ttl_seconds: Time-to-live in seconds. None means no expiration.

in-memory-cache.title: InMemoryCache
in-memory-cache.description:
Default in-memory cache implementation with LRU eviction.

Designed for asyncio (single-threaded).
For multi-threaded environments, implement a custom CacheAdapter
with appropriate locking.

Features:
- TTL (time-to-live) support per entry using monotonic clock
- LRU (Least Recently Used) eviction when max_entries reached
- No external dependencies

Args:
    max_entries: Maximum number of entries to cache. When exceeded,
                least recently used entry is evicted. Default: 100.

Example:
    cache = InMemoryCache(max_entries=50)
    cache.set("key1", {"data": "value"}, ttl_seconds=600)
    value = cache.get("key1")  # Returns {"data": "value"}

domains-resolver.title: DomainsResolver
domains-resolver.description: Type alias for a callable that resolves allowed Auth0 domains at request time. Used in multi-tenant setups.

domains-resolver-context.title: DomainsResolverContext
domains-resolver-context.description:
Context passed to domains resolver functions.

Attributes:
    request_url: The URL the API request was made to (optional)
    request_headers: Request headers dict (e.g., Host, X-Forwarded-Host) (optional)
    unverified_iss: The issuer claim from the unverified token

properties.name: request_url
properties.description: The URL of the incoming request.

properties.name: request_headers
properties.description: The headers of the incoming request as a dictionary.

properties.name: unverified_iss
properties.description: The issuer claim extracted from the token before signature verification. Use this to decide which tenant to allow.

domains-resolver-error.title: DomainsResolverError
domains-resolver-error.description: Error raised when domains resolver function fails.

configuration-error.title: ConfigurationError
configuration-error.description: Error raised when SDK configuration is invalid.

examples.code:
from auth0_fastapi_api import Auth0FastAPI

# audience is required — omitting it raises ConfigurationError
auth0 = Auth0FastAPI(domain='YOUR_AUTH0_DOMAIN')
